talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Frederick National Lab / BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics
talk_metadata.talk_title: KRAS inhibitor clinical and preclinical updates
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have three brand new first-in-class compounds already in clinical trials. And I'm not going to show clinical data today because they're all pursuing dose escalation"
Speaker said: "we haven't got the clinical readouts yet, so we can't say how effective they are"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: KRAS wild-type amplification leading to pathway reactivation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted: "after over 300 days on therapy, we observed radiographic PD with acquired KRAS wild-type amplification"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker mentioned: "one patient remained on therapy after over 15 months despite only disease stabilization"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: KRAS G12D inhibition combined with EGFR blockade
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all-comer PDAC patients without stratifying for KRAS amplification status

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker admitted the compound does not fully cover wild-type KRAS amplification, limiting efficacy
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on EGFR combination to achieve depth of response

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed preclinical tumor regressions with 3034 monotherapy but concluded that monotherapy alone only yields disease stabilization in patients

key_data_gaps: No biomarker‑stratified analysis for KRAS amplification

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for prospective KRAS amplification testing to select patients who may benefit from 3034 monotherapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we saw radiographic PD with acquired KRAS wild-type amplification"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate ctDNA and tissue sequencing to flag KRAS amplification and guide enrollment

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop next‑generation KRAS inhibitors that retain activity against KRAS amplification
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted the limitation of 3034 against wild‑type KRAS amplification
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model mutant‑wild‑type KRAS dynamics to prioritize compounds with broader coverage

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify optimal upstream partners (e.g., SHP2, MET) beyond EGFR to overcome resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed EGFR combo and mentioned MET as a potential partner
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic pathways using network analysis of resistance data

cited_competitors.name: sotorasib
cited_competitors.context: approved G12C inhibitor referenced as existing therapy

cited_competitors.name: adagrasib
cited_competitors.context: approved G12C inhibitor mentioned in context of combination with EGFR antibodies

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal dose of 3034 to achieve EC90 across all KRAS mutants?
Can KRAS amplification be prevented or delayed with earlier combination therapy?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Pan‑Ras tricomplex inhibitors and clinical progress
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we did not see any new safety signals in this particular study"
Speaker said: "we see very nice activity now beyond G12, as predicted by the preclinical data"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Amplification of mutant KRAS allele leading to pathway re‑activation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted: "we often see amplification of the mutant KRAS allele in patients on duraxon rasib"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker highlighted durable responses in a subset of patients but overall relapse
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker mentioned manageable Grade 3 on‑pathway toxicities but no liver signals

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Pan‑Ras inhibition in second‑line pancreatic cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Includes KRAS‑wild‑type patients without separate efficacy cohort

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Dependence on cyclophilin A levels; loss of cyclophilin could confer resistance (early data shows no change yet)
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Potential tolerability ceiling due to on‑target effects in normal tissues

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented preclinical tumor regressions with deruxon rasib monotherapy yet concluded that monotherapy leads to rapid relapse in patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate cyclophilin A expression and KRAS amplification into patient selection
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker asked about cyclophilin loss and noted early data shows no change
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can combine proteomic and genomic data to flag patients at risk of cyclophilin‑mediated resistance

crispro_opportunity.description: Explore SHP2 or MET inhibitors as upstream partners to enhance durability
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed EGFR combo and mentioned MET as a potential partner
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic upstream targets based on pathway rewiring after KRAS inhibition

crispro_opportunity.description: Design next‑gen pan‑Ras molecules that retain activity against KRAS amplification
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that 3034 loses activity against wild‑type KRAS amplification
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model mutant‑wild‑type KRAS dynamics to guide scaffold optimization

cited_competitors.name: sotorasib
cited_competitors.context: Approved G12C inhibitor referenced as existing standard

cited_competitors.name: adagrasib
cited_competitors.context: Approved G12C inhibitor mentioned in combination with EGFR antibodies

cited_competitors.name: RMC‑6236 (Deruxon‑rasib)
cited_competitors.context: Company’s own pan‑Ras candidate discussed throughout

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of pan‑Ras inhibitor with chemotherapy in frontline PDAC?
Can cyclophilin A levels predict toxicity or efficacy?
